summary: Stage 2 — Additional Cost 4-step Approval Chain (PP→PH→GM→Client) end-to-end tested. Backend: 9/9 pytest cases pass — single-item happy path, PH reject (rejected_at_step=planning_head), GM reject (rejected_at_step=general_manager), state-machine guards (ph/gm approve only from correct prior state), section-level batch (submit-for-review / ph-approve / gm-approve all moved 2/2 items), and the new /api/cre/additional-costs endpoint (shape {rows:[...]} with project_name/client_name enrichment, 403 for non-CRE planning role, GM-approved row appears in queue). Frontend: CRE Board → Additional Costs tab visible with all 3 sub-tabs (pending_client, client_approved, all), rows grouped by project with View Project link. ProjectDetail → Final Estimate → Additional sub-tab renders all inline pipeline buttons (submit-review-*, ph-approve-*, ph-reject-*, resubmit-review-*) plus ungrouped toolbar batch buttons (ungrouped-submit-review, ungrouped-ph-approve). Status badges 'Pending Planning Head', 'Pending Client', 'Rejected at …' render. Legacy 'Send to Client' button is removed (0 in DOM).

issues: React hydration warning: '<div> cannot be a child of <tbody>'. Non-blocking but should be cleaned up — table row contains a <div> child where only <tr>/<td> is valid HTML.

action_items: (Optional) Fix invalid HTML in ProjectDetail.jsx around line 3753 — <div className='max-w-[180...'> nested inside a <tbody>. Will eventually break React 19 hydration.
critical_code_review_comments: Approval state machine guards work correctly: ph-approve requires ph_review, gm-approve requires gm_review, ph-reject/gm-reject require non-empty reason (else 400).

GM approval auto-sets client_approval_status=pending_client AND client_approval_sent_at/by, so the client portal and CRE Board pick the row up without an extra explicit 'send to client' action — confirmed by /api/cre/additional-costs returning the GM-approved row immediately.

/api/cre/additional-costs correctly scopes by team.cre or created_by for CRE role and returns 403 for non-CRE/non-superadmin (verified with planning user). Response shape {rows:[…with project_name, client_name…]} matches CRE Board's consumer.

Frontend correctly renders 'Resubmit for Review' button on a rejected row (resubmit-review-* testid present), and the backend allows submit-for-review when current status is 'rejected' (state machine accepts None/created/rejected).

Section-batch endpoints only act on items whose approval_status matches the expected stage (submit picks None/created/rejected, ph-approve picks ph_review only, gm-approve picks gm_review only), so calling them is idempotent and safe.
test_credentials: admin@constructionos.com / Demo@1234 (used for super_admin acting as all roles), planning@constructionos.com / Demo@1234 (used to confirm 403 on /cre/additional-costs).
seed_data_creation: Created additional_costs on proj_be3a8542700b (Mrs. Abinaya). Multiple TEST_AC_4step_* and TEST_SEC_* rows now exist in various states (ph_review, rejected, awaiting_client). Two probe sections were created with section_ids 'sec_test_<ts>'. proj_f66ab2fcaa58 (Swathi) is globally locked (returns 423 on POST /additional-costs because some prior item is client-approved) — fixture now auto-cycles to find an unlocked project.
context_for_next_testing_agent: If running tests again, the fixture in test_addition_4step_approval.py probes projects sequentially and picks the first unlocked one (currently proj_be3a8542700b). Login uses session cookies (requests.Session()). The /api/cre/additional-costs endpoint sits inside /app/backend/routes/projects.py at lines 4949-4984. Frontend tab is in CREBoard.jsx around line 816 (TabsTrigger) and TabsContent at line 1310. Inline pipeline buttons in ProjectDetail.jsx render only when the user clicks Final Estimate → Additional sub-tab (NOT a separate top-level 'Additional Work' tab as the spec text suggests).
